title: Obtain FCC Part 15 / Part 90 certification for LoRa hardware sold in the US (US915 band)
description: Required before any commercial sale

title: Obtain CE marking (RED directive 2014/53/EU) for EU market
description: Covers radio, safety, EMC for ESP32+LoRa hardware

title: Obtain ISED RSS-247 / RSS-Gen certification for Canada
description: CA915 band and confirm duty-cycle limits

title: Obtain MIC (TELEC) Type Approval for Japan
description: 920 MHz J-band, separate from FCC, required for JP sales

title: Confirm ACMA RCM mark requirements for Australia/NZ
description: AU915 band and file declaration of conformity

title: Document per-region firmware builds
description: US915 / AU915 / EU868 / AS923 / IN865 / RU864 — lock frequency and ERP automatically at flash time via region flag

title: Implement hard ETSI EN 300 220 duty-cycle enforcement in firmware
description: 1% sub-band limit on EU868, log and throttle transmissions automatically

title: Add FCC/ETSI ID watermark to PCB silkscreen and firmware version string
description: For FCC labeling compliance

title: Verify LoRa TX power limits per region in lora_config.h
description: EU868 max 14 dBm ERP on most sub-bands; US915 max 30 dBm EIRP (with license implications)

title: Conduct EMC pre-compliance testing
description: Radiated/conducted emissions before final FCC/CE submission — catches failures cheaply

title: Research South Korea KCC (KC mark) and Brazil ANATEL requirements
description: For potential expansion

title: Research India WPC type approval for 865–867 MHz LoRa band
description: Before India launch

title: Draft export control classification
description: EAR99 or ECCN 5A991 for the hardware — LoRa encryption may require ECCN 5E002 self-classification

title: Create a 'regulatory matrix' doc
description: One row per country, columns = band, ERP limit, duty cycle, certification required, status

title: Clarify whether LoRa at 868/915 MHz needs Listen-Before-Talk (LBT)
description: In EU/Japan and implement in firmware if so

title: Build a full zero-to-first-beacon onboarding flow
description: Hardware unboxing → pairing → first position seen on map in <5 minutes

title: Replace default Leaflet tile styling with offline vector maps
description: MapTiler GL JS or Mapbox — critical for wilderness/off-grid use cases where tile CDN is unavailable

title: Add human-readable error messages for all packet decode failures
description: 'Bad CRC' means nothing to end users — provide helpful context

title: Implement a guided setup wizard in the peer-map app
description: Select region → validate hardware → test TX/RX → confirm first fix

title: Build a 'no hardware? no problem' fully simulated demo mode
description: Fake GPS tracks, configurable node count, and SOS simulation — critical for sales demos

title: Add battery level indicator to the peer-map UI
description: Users need to know when a beacon is about to die in the field

title: Implement push notifications (PWA + native) for SOS events
description: A silent map is useless when someone needs rescue

title: Add configurable alert thresholds
description: Alert me if beacon X hasn't transmitted in N minutes

title: Implement device naming / nicknames
description: Alice's T-Beam not a raw device ID hex

title: Add a track history playback slider with time scrubbing
description: Users need to reconstruct routes after an event

title: Implement map clustering for 20+ simultaneous nodes
description: UI doesn't become unusable at scale

title: Add dark/satellite/topo map layer switching in the peer-map
description: With saved preference

title: Build a printable / shareable track summary
description: PDF export with map screenshot + GPX stats

title: Add a voice/haptic SOS confirmation step
description: Prevent accidental triggers from the mobile app

title: Create a 'health dashboard' panel
description: Last-seen time per device, packet loss rate, RSSI history, battery trend

title: Implement LEP v2 packet format with AES-128-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption
description: V1 transmits plaintext lat/lon over LoRa, a privacy catastrophe for production

title: Design and implement a key distribution / pairing scheme for v2
description: Pre-shared group key or ECDH handshake via BLE for initial pairing

title: Add replay-attack protection to LEP v2
description: Sequence counter + timestamp window check at receiver — without it anyone can rebroadcast old positions

title: Conduct a formal security threat model audit (STRIDE)
description: Covering full stack: firmware → LoRa air interface → BLE → web dashboard → backend → cloud

title: Write and publish a privacy policy
description: Clearly state: what position data is stored, for how long, who can access it, and the consent model — required for GDPR/App Store

title: Implement GDPR Article 17 'right to erasure' in the backend
description: User can delete all their stored position history

title: Add explicit consent flow for location sharing in the mobile app
description: Sharing your location with group [X] with clear opt-out

title: Store encryption keys in ESP32 NVS (flash) with secure boot enabled
description: Never hard-code keys in firmware source

title: Enable ESP32 Secure Boot V2 and Flash Encryption in production firmware builds
description: Prevent firmware extraction or replacement

title: Implement certificate pinning in the mobile app
description: For any cloud API calls — prevents MITM attacks on the device

title: Set up a vulnerability disclosure / bug bounty program
description: Security@domain contact and documented SLA for fixes

title: Commission external penetration test of the full stack
description: Before public launch — covers web app, API, BLE attack surface, LoRa replay

title: Implement anti-stalking safeguards
description: Randomized device ID rotation on a schedule so passive observers can't track a beacon they're not paired with

title: Add rate limiting and anomaly detection to the backend relay
description: Flag devices transmitting far more than their duty cycle allows

title: Implement GDPR data processing agreements (DPAs)
description: For any third-party services (PostHog, AWS, etc.) that process EU user data

title: Design a producible PCB that integrates ESP32, LoRa radio, GNSS module, USB-C charging, and LiPo management
description: Moving beyond T-Beam dev boards

title: Identify and qualify 2+ contract manufacturers (CM) for PCB assembly
description: Production run of ≥500 units as pilot

title: Certify the custom hardware for RoHS compliance
description: Restriction of hazardous substances — required for EU sale

title: Ensure WEEE registration in EU countries where you sell
description: Mandatory for electronics producers, even small ones

title: Validate antenna design and SMA connector / PCB trace antenna
description: For regulatory-compliant gain — measured in an anechoic chamber or via certified test lab

title: Design protective enclosure (IP67 minimum) for field use
description: GPS beacons left in rain/snow need environmental sealing

title: Create a Bill of Materials (BOM) with dual-source alternatives
description: For every critical component — chip shortage resilience

title: Implement ESD protection on all external-facing I/O pins
description: USB, antenna connector, programming header

title: Evaluate solar charging integration for long-duration deployments
description: Small panel + LiPo is key for SAR / wilderness applications

title: Test T-Beam and Heltec boards at temperature extremes
description: -20°C to +70°C and document operational limits

title: Design a hardware 'Getting Started Kit' SKU
description: Beacon + pre-programmed firmware + USB-C cable + quick start card — lowers barrier to first purchase

title: Implement hardware versioning in firmware
description: Device reads its own board revision from flash and selects correct pin map automatically

title: Implement hardware watchdog timer (WDT) across all firmware task loops
description: A crashed node in the field cannot be manually rebooted

title: Implement OTA (Over-The-Air) firmware update
description: Via WiFi provisioning or BLE from the mobile app with signature verification

title: Implement adaptive duty-cycle governor
description: Firmware tracks cumulative TX time per sub-band and refuses to transmit when approaching regional legal limit

title: Add GNSS almanac/ephemeris caching using battery-backed RTC
description: Dramatically reduce cold-start TTFF from 60s+ to <10s

title: Implement CSMA/CA (listen-before-talk) for LoRa transmission
description: Reduce collision probability in dense deployments

title: Add configurable beacon interval in firmware via BLE GATT characteristic
description: Allow operators to trade battery life vs. update frequency

title: Implement deep-sleep scheduler
description: Sleep → GNSS fix → encode → TX → sleep, targeting <100µA average current for multi-week battery life

title: Write and publish formal spec for LEP v2 (encrypted)
description: Full wire format, field semantics, CRC scheme, and version negotiation

title: Add LRM1 mesh frame collision detection and TTL countdown
description: Prevent routing loops in large mesh networks

title: Implement satellite uplink gateway firmware (Iridium SBD / Kinéis)
description: As an optional module — the natural next transport layer beyond LoRa range

title: Port LEP encode/decode to C (from TypeScript shared/)
description: Firmware and cloud share byte-identical codec — prevents subtle endianness bugs

title: Add a factory test mode
description: Firmware writes known packet, verifies CRC, pings BLE, reports GNSS lock — QC step during manufacturing

title: Profile and optimize deep-sleep current on T-Beam
description: Characterize GPS module, LoRa radio, and ESP32 core separately using a power analyzer

title: Comply with Apple App Store guideline 5.5 (location background modes)
description: Must demonstrate 'always-on' location is genuinely necessary for safety use case, not just convenience

title: Write a detailed App Store privacy nutrition label for iOS
description: Enumerate all data collected (precise location, device ID) and their purposes

title: Configure Google Play data safety form
description: Declare location data, transmission to third parties (cloud relay), retention period

title: Set up Fastlane CI pipeline for automated iOS TestFlight + Android Play Internal track distribution
description: On each main branch push

title: Implement in-app review prompt
description: Apple SKStoreReviewRequest / Google Play In-App Review API after a successful first-beacon session

title: Handle iOS background location permission gracefully
description: Show educational interstitial BEFORE requesting permission, explain the safety purpose

title: Test Capacitor app on iOS 16, 17, 18 and Android 12, 13, 14, 15
description: BLE and location APIs change between versions

title: Submit to Apple's emergency-use app exemptions if applicable
description: Certain safety-critical apps can bypass standard review delays

title: Build an iOS widget (WidgetKit) showing last known positions
description: Useful for Search and Rescue team leaders

title: Implement App Clips (iOS) / Instant Apps (Android)
description: For viewing a shared track link without a full install

title: Implement API authentication
description: All cloud relay endpoints require a signed JWT or API key — unauthenticated packet relay enables position data leakage

title: Add rate limiting on all public endpoints
description: Protect relay against packet flooding and credential stuffing

title: Define and implement data retention policy in the backend
description: Position data auto-deleted after N days unless user opts into longer storage

title: Deploy backend in at least 2 AWS regions
description: E.g. eu-west-1 + us-east-1 for GDPR data residency and latency reasons

title: Implement automated database backups with point-in-time recovery (PITR)
description: Losing position history during a SAR operation is unacceptable

title: Set up comprehensive observability
description: Structured logging (CloudWatch / Datadog), distributed tracing (X-Ray / OTEL), uptime alerting (<1 min detection)

title: Write and publish an SLA
description: Commit to uptime (99.9%? 99.5%?), define 'service degraded' vs 'outage', and an incident communication channel

title: Complete the Terraform IaC for all production infrastructure
description: No manual console clicks in production, every resource version-controlled

title: Implement WebSocket / SSE endpoint for real-time position streaming
description: To the peer-map without polling

title: Add a GDPR-compliant data export endpoint
description: User can download their full position history as GPX/JSON/CSV

title: Evaluate edge/CDN caching strategy for the offline map tile server
description: To reduce cost at scale

title: Implement chaos engineering tests
description: E.g. Gremlin or AWS FIS to validate resilience before production traffic

title: Achieve ≥80% unit test coverage on shared/packet encode/decode
description: A codec bug silently corrupts all location data across the entire system

title: Build automated integration tests
description: Encode a packet in TypeScript, decode on firmware C side, verify bit-identical round-trip

title: Conduct multi-environment LoRa range tests
description: Urban dense, suburban, open field, dense forest — document RSSI, SNR, packet loss per SF/BW combination

title: Build mesh scalability simulation
description: 100+ virtual nodes with realistic duty cycles, verify deduplication and relay convergence time

title: Run WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit on peer-map web app
description: Fix all critical violations

title: Add E2E browser tests (Playwright) for the peer-map critical path
description: Paste hex → decode → pin appears on map → SOS styling

title: Test offline map fallback
description: Disable network in Capacitor app and verify the map still renders and beacons still plot using cached tiles

title: Conduct formal battery profiling protocol
description: Measure µA in deep sleep, active GNSS, active TX separately on T-Beam and Heltec — document in docs/POWER_METRICS.md

title: Add firmware fuzz testing for the LEP decoder
description: Malformed bytes from a rogue LoRa transmitter should not crash the receiver

title: Build a hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) test bench
description: Automated overnight soak test verifying TX/RX, WDT recovery, and OTA on actual hardware

title: Set up Lighthouse CI in the GitHub Actions pipeline
description: Track PWA score, performance, and accessibility on every PR

title: Define the primary go-to-market segment
description: Outdoor recreation (hiking/skiing), SAR teams, military/defense, or maritime — each has different price tolerance and regulatory requirements

title: Set pricing
description: Hardware kit (one-time) + SaaS relay subscription model — model unit economics for a 100-node team vs. a solo hiker

title: Create a B2B enterprise tier
description: Dedicated relay server, SLA, custom domain, SSO/SAML, volume discounts, and an account manager contact

title: Build a self-serve sign-up flow and billing integration
description: Stripe — you cannot manually invoice every customer at scale

title: Define a free tier that showcases value without giving everything away
description: E.g. unlimited hardware + BLE-only, paid for LoRa relay + cloud history

title: Research government procurement and GSA schedule
description: For US federal SAR agencies — major revenue but requires SAM.gov registration and CAGE code

title: Explore reseller / channel partner program
description: With outdoor retailers (REI, Decathlon) and tactical distributors

title: Create a 'developer API' tier for third parties
description: Building on top of LEP — opens a platform ecosystem and generates data network effects

title: Model hardware COGS at 1K, 10K, 100K unit volumes
description: Understand when ODM vs. own PCB design crosses over on margin

title: Hire or contract a regulatory/compliance consultant
description: Experienced in FCC/CE radio certification before hardware production — mistakes here cost 6+ months

title: Engage a lawyer specializing in data privacy
description: GDPR/CCPA to review the privacy policy, consent flows, and DPAs before EU/US launch

title: Recruit a hardware engineer with embedded RF/LoRa PCB experience
description: To own the custom board design and certification process

title: Set up a Customer Success function
description: Could be founder-led initially: respond to support tickets within 24h, build a knowledge base / FAQ

title: Establish a beta tester community
description: SAR volunteers, mountain rescue teams, or hiking clubs — real field feedback before general launch

title: Create a contributor guide and code of conduct
description: For the open-source components — sets expectations for community contributions

title: Identify and reach out to potential advisors
description: Ex-Garmin/SPOT hardware lead, ex-Iridium product person, a SAR coordinator, and a VC who has funded safety tech

title: Define an on-call rotation for production incidents
description: A founder getting woken at 3am every incident is not sustainable

title: Run formal user research sessions
description: 5–8 participants with SAR coordinators: observe how they currently track team members in the field

title: Add a mandatory disclaimer in the app and hardware documentation
description: This device is NOT a certified distress beacon (PLB/EPIRB). Do not rely on it as your sole emergency device.

title: Implement SOS false-alarm mitigation
description: Require confirmation gesture (hold for 3s + PIN) before transmitting SOS flag — false alerts consume SAR resources

title: Design an emergency escalation workflow
description: SOS flag received → notify all group members → optionally forward to a nominated emergency contact via SMS/email

title: Integrate with national SAR coordination systems
description: Where APIs are available (e.g. IMRCC maritime, US Coast Guard) as a 'bonus channel' — not a replacement for 406 MHz PLB

title: Implement 'last-known-good' position broadcast
description: When a beacon stops transmitting, relay its last position + timestamp to the group map until it resumes

title: Add an 'I'm OK' check-in button with a configurable interval timer
description: Missed check-ins trigger an alert to the group, similar to a dead-man's switch

title: Work with a wilderness medicine or SAR organization
description: Validate the SOS workflow in realistic rescue scenarios and document recommended protocols

title: Obtain product liability insurance before commercial sales
description: Essential for a device used in life-safety situations

title: Add a 'low battery SOS pre-warning'
description: Triggers at 10% battery: alert group that beacon will go dark soon so they can respond

title: Implement i18next (or similar) in the peer-map web and Capacitor apps
description: All user-facing strings externalized to JSON locale files

title: Translate UI into at least 7 languages
description: English, French, German, Spanish, Czech, Norwegian/Swedish, Japanese

title: Ensure coordinate display respects locale
description: Some regions use comma as decimal separator in lat/lon — use Intl.NumberFormat consistently

title: Localize date/time formats and time zones
description: ISO 8601 vs. DD/MM/YYYY vs. MM/DD/YYYY throughout the app

title: Add RTL layout support for Arabic and Hebrew markets
description: Affects the peer-map and any onboarding UI

title: Localize map defaults by region
description: EU users should see EU868 band as default, US users see US915, etc. — infer from browser locale or explicit setting

title: Translate hardware quick-start card into 6+ languages
description: For inclusion in the international retail box

title: Draft Terms of Service
description: No-warranty for safety-critical use, prohibition of covert tracking, user data ownership, acceptable use policy

title: Register the company entity and trademark 'Location Emitter'
description: Before public launch — prevents squatting

title: Audit all open-source dependencies for license compatibility
description: Apache 2.0 / MIT are fine; GPL v2 in firmware could require firmware source disclosure on product sale

title: Register with local data protection authority
description: As a data processor — required in some EU countries even for small companies processing location data

title: File provisional patent application if applicable
description: If the LEP v2 encryption+mesh approach is novel enough — blocks competitors and is useful for YC/investor narrative

title: Create a responsible disclosure policy for the radio protocol
description: Document clearly that LoRa is not licensed for 406 MHz EPIRB bands — prevents dangerous misuse

title: Create a channel-specific hardware End User License Agreement (EULA)
description: For enterprise B2B customers covering IP ownership of customer data

title: Research export control (ITAR/EAR) implications
description: Of selling encrypted location-sharing hardware to non-US buyers — may need BIS classification ruling

title: Pick a final product name and domain
description: 'locationEmmiter' (note typo) is a dev repo name, not a brand. Register a proper .com domain

title: Build a high-converting landing page
description: Hero video of real field use, feature breakdown, pricing tiers, and email capture waitlist

title: Create 3 target customer personas with pain/gain maps
description: Weekend hiker, SAR team lead, military unit commander — all have different messaging

title: Produce a 90-second product demo video
description: Full flow: hardware → map → SOS — for website, YC video, and social

title: Establish presence in key communities
description: Meshtastic Discord, APRS forums, r/amateurradio, ATAK user community, wilderness medicine groups

title: Write SEO-targeted blog content
description: 'Best LoRa tracker for hiking', 'offline GPS for SAR teams', 'mesh communication without cell signal'

title: Apply to outdoor/safety-tech accelerators and challenge grants
description: DARPA SBIR, EU Horizon, outdoor industry innovation funds for non-dilutive funding

title: Partner with 2–3 SAR organizations for free unit trial
description: In exchange for testimonial and field data — strongest possible social proof for the target market
